questions.question: A wild banana Musa sikkimensis, that was recently seen in news, is native to which region?
questions.explanation: Researchers at Nagaland University studied the genetic diversity of Musa sikkimensis, a wild banana native to the Eastern Himalayas and Northeast India. Known as Darjeeling or Sikkim banana, it is a wild seeded species with traits for disease resistance, environmental stress tolerance, and climate adaptability, crucial for future breeding. Though not widely cultivated for fruit, it strengthens crop resilience, food security, and sustainable agriculture. Nagaland, part of the Indo Burma biodiversity hotspot, hosts diverse indigenous banana genotypes at risk due to deforestation and human activity.

questions.question: Where was India’s first Samriddhi Centre inaugurated?
questions.explanation: Union Communications Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia inaugurated India’s first Samriddhi Centre at Umri village in Guna district, Madhya Pradesh. The centre is an integrated rural service hub designed to improve service delivery and digital access in villages. It provides multiple services like healthcare, education, banking, agriculture support, and other citizen services at one place. It is part of the Samriddh Gram Phygital Services Pilot Project, combining physical infrastructure with digital technology. The centre aims to ensure seamless and efficient service delivery in rural areas.

questions.question: The “True Cyber Sakhi” initiative has been launched by which organization?

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

options.label: D
options.text: National Commission for Protection of Child Rights

questions.explanation: The National Commission for Women (NCW) has launched “True Cyber Sakhi”, a digital safety toolkit aimed at empowering women in the online space. The initiative focuses on enhancing awareness, resilience, and protection against cyber threats and online harassment. It is designed to educate women about safe internet practices, including identifying cyber fraud, phishing, and digital abuse. The toolkit promotes digital literacy and self-protection skills, enabling women to navigate the online world confidently and securely.

questions.question: Which state government has declared cancer a notifiable disease to strengthen surveillance of non-communicable diseases in April 2026?
questions.explanation: The Telangana government has declared cancer a notifiable disease to strengthen surveillance of non-communicable diseases. All healthcare facilities, including government and private hospitals, diagnostic labs, and clinics, must report cancer cases within one month of diagnosis. The move aims to create a centralized cancer registry for accurate estimation of incidence and prevalence. It will support evidence-based policymaking, better planning of palliative care, and improved resource management. The Mehdi Nawaz Jung Institute of Oncology and Regional Cancer Centre has been designated as the Centre of Excellence for data validation and coordination with the national registry.

questions.question: Which state government launched a major AI literacy programme for nearly 2 million government school students in April 2026?
questions.explanation: The Telangana Government launched a statewide initiative to provide Artificial Intelligence (AI) literacy to nearly 2 million government school students. The programme is implemented in partnership with Amazon (Amazon Future Engineer) and Pi Jam Foundation. Around 2,000 laptops were distributed to Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalayas (KGBVs) and other schools to bridge the digital divide. From 2025–26, students of Classes 5–9 will study a structured curriculum designed by SCERT Telangana. The curriculum focuses on computational thinking, design, and digital citizenship, beyond basic computer skills.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of SARPA app?
questions.explanation: Kerala is upgrading its snake-response platform amid rising snakebite cases during a heat wave. The SARPA (Snake Awareness, Rescue and Protection App) helps report snake sightings and coordinate rescue operations. The upgraded version will integrate doctors and snake experts for real-time emergency guidance. It aims to reduce fatalities by improving response time and first-aid decision-making. The app will provide dedicated login access for doctors to guide responders remotely. The initiative strengthens coordination between rescue teams and healthcare facilities.

questions.question: Which state has become the first in India to introduce a 10-year ‘warranty’ for rural drinking water schemes?
questions.explanation: Uttar Pradesh has become the first state to introduce a 10-year ‘warranty’ (Operations & Maintenance commitment) for rural drinking water schemes under the Jal Jeevan Mission. Under the Jal Arpan initiative, ownership and management of water supply schemes have been transferred to Gram Panchayats. The move strengthens decentralised governance and community participation in water management. The state has expanded water quality monitoring infrastructure with district, state, and mobile laboratories (NABL-accredited).

questions.question: Virli Khandar excavation site, that was recently seen in news, is located in which state?
questions.explanation: The Virli Khandar excavation is an ongoing archaeological study at a megalithic burial site in Bhandara district, Maharashtra. The site dates roughly from 1000 BCE to 300 CE, with key evidence around 500 BCE. It reveals distinct burial architecture, including stone circles and menhirs (large upright standing stones used in prehistoric monuments). Around 69 burials have been documented, with selected excavations underway.

questions.question: Which state government launched Operation Kalanemi to expose and eliminate fraudulent spiritual leaders who mislead the public under the guise of faith?
questions.explanation: In Rudraprayag, authorities have launched Operation Kalanemi to ensure safety during the Kedarnath Dham Yatra. The operation has been initiated under directions of District Magistrate Vishal Mishra. Its objective is to identify and take action against anti-social elements posing as sadhus in the pilgrimage area. Police have been directed to verify suspicious individuals and maintain strict surveillance. The operation aims to ensure law and order, pilgrim safety, and a hassle-free yatra experience.

questions.question: The Aarogya Maitri Aid Cube, recently deployed in Jamaica, is associated with which initiative?
questions.explanation: India deployed Aarogya Maitri portable healthcare infrastructure in Jamaica to strengthen humanitarian diplomacy. The initiative boosts India–Caribbean ties under engagement with CARICOM. It operates within India’s Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief (HADR) framework. Aarogya Maitri Health Cube is the world’s first portable hospital, designed indigenously under Project BHISHM. It includes medical equipment like a mini-ICU, operation theatre, cooking station, food, water, power generator, blood test equipment, and an X-ray machine. This innovative hospital can provide essential healthcare in remote and high-altitude areas.
